payload.description: Add report command rolling up existing events into an honest value summary (Stage 1)

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The rejected-round metric reads a field that imported verdicts cannot contain, and its tests never exercise rejection.
Bypass disclosure causality and the claimed acknowledgement signal do not match repository evidence semantics.
The documentation task does not declare the exact generated-reference files and source map that must change.

findings.summary: `rejected_rounds` inspects `review_result_imported.payload.verdict.outcome`, but the frozen verdict schema forbids that field; rejection is recorded on `review_round_closed.payload.outcome`, so this metric will remain zero for real rejected rounds.

The proposed set-based bypass calculation ignores append-order causality: a disclosure before a later bypass on the same change incorrectly clears that bypass, contradicting the existing `gate_bypass_disclosure` contract.

An open finding only proves that a reviewer raised it and no disposition was recorded; rendering it as something the user “acknowledged” fabricates a user action absent from the event stream and violates the plan’s honesty law.

Task 9 names a nonexistent `docs/reference/` location and omits exact generated-doc scope, including `docs/cli-reference.md` and `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`; without a `report` exit-code-map entry, regeneration documents the wrong generic 0/1 exits instead of the command’s 0/3 contract.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The rejected-round metric omits the actual execution-failure outcome emitted by the review lifecycle.
The proposed outcome predicate would systematically underreport review cost.
The plan declares all 11 intended source, test, generated-doc, generator, plan, and private ledger files.

findings.summary: Count `execution_failed`, not nonexistent `failed`: review round closure emits `approved`, `rejected`, or `execution_failed`, so the proposed predicate and test omit crashed, stale-target, incomplete-governance, and other failed rounds from the cost report.

checklist.note: All read fields verified to exist with claimed semantics; only imprecision is a harmless dead literal 'failed' in rejected_rounds set (real vocab is {approved,rejected,execution_failed}).

Order-aware per-change undisclosed-bypass logic correct; open-findings uses full-stream disposition (no boundary fabrication, no user-action claim); _usage_tokens never guesses; value_report.py imports only core (preserves d-core-is-base).
All 11 touched files declared incl. generated doc, generator exit-map, registration, tests, plan, and private ledgers; AGENTS.md correctly excluded.

findings.summary: rejected_rounds set {rejected, failed} lists a nonexistent outcome value: review_round_closed only emits {approved, rejected, execution_failed}, so 'failed' is dead and execution_failed rounds are silently uncounted.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The proposed model, implementation, tests, rendering, and review oracle consistently cover the renamed metric.
The interpretation of execution_failed is not honest for every path that emits that outcome.
The metric change remains within the declared file scope.

findings.summary: The plan classifies every execution_failed closure as wasted rework and renders it as “failed to execute,” but this outcome also represents incomplete human quorum where passing automated evidence remains usable. Counting that normal mixed-quorum path as failed execution fabricates rework and violates the report’s honesty contract.

findings.summary: The plan classifies every execution_failed closure as wasted rework and renders it as “failed to execute,” but this outcome also represents incomplete human quorum where passing automated evidence remains usable. Counting that normal mixed-quorum path as failed execution fabricates rework and violates the report’s honesty contract.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: Two report metrics can contradict the documented honesty and window contracts.
The implementation stays within the assigned target.
Finding identity is aggregated without its change scope.
Cross-change ID collisions and date-only upper bounds produce incorrect counts.
The documented --until date behavior does not match runtime behavior.

findings.summary: Finding IDs are deduplicated globally instead of by change. A disposition for F1 on one change therefore clears an unrelated F1 on another change and multiple same-named fixes are undercounted; legacy code_review_failed events commonly use such short IDs. Track findings as (change_id, id).

A date-only --until value parses as midnight, so --until 2026-07-10 excludes every event later on July 10 despite the CLI and generated documentation promising events “on/before this ISO date.” Normalize date-only upper bounds to the end of that day or document and require timestamp semantics.

/bin/zsh -lc "PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 .venv/bin/python - <<'PY'
from super_harness.core.parse_ts import parse_ts
print('until_bound', parse_ts('2026-07-10'))
print('same_day_noon_included', parse_ts('2026-07-10T12:00:00Z') <= parse_ts('2026-07-10'))
from super_harness.engineering.value_report import _finding_counts
from super_harness.core.events import Event, Actor

def ev(eid, change, findings=(), prior=()):
    return Event(event_id=eid,type='review_result_imported',change_id=change,timestamp='2026-07-10T00:00:00Z',actor=Actor(type='agent',identifier='codex'),framework='plain',payload={'reviewer':'code-reviewer','verdict':{'findings':[{'id': x} for x in findings], 'prior_findings':[{'id': x, 'disposition': d} for x,d in prior]}})
stream=[ev('e1','a',findings=['F1']),ev('e2','a',prior=[('F1','resolved')]),ev('e3','b',findings=['F1'])]
print('cross_change_counts', _finding_counts(stream, stream))
PY

checklist.note: Every metric verified against real emit sites; only 'rejected' counted; _usage_tokens None on unknown shapes; open-undisposed uses full-stream disposition; honesty law verified live.
All 8 reviewed files within declared 11-file scope; additions only.
Clean, DRY, typed; d-core-is-base preserved. Lone nit: inconsistent defensive guarding between _review_cost (guards receipt) and _dispositions/_raised_ids (does not guard verdict) - see CLDX-001.

Empty/missing stream, malformed lines, None payloads, unparseable timestamps, usage=None, window boundaries handled. Sole gap: non-dict verdict crashes report (CLDX-001), unreachable via real emitters.
docs/cli-reference.md regenerates byte-identically; report section params + exit codes 0/3 correct; _EXIT_CODES entry matches actual exits.

findings.summary: _dispositions and _raised_ids call verdict.get(...) without an isinstance-dict guard (unlike _review_cost's receipt guard), so a parseable event with a non-dict 'verdict' crashes the report with an uncaught AttributeError, contradicting the never-crash design; unreachable via real emitters.
